subject: [PATCH v1 02/17] fuse: prepare for passthrough of inode operations

reviews.author: Darrick Wong
reviews.summary: Reviewer Darrick Wong noted that using FUSE_READ and FUSE_WRITE in the ops_mask is inefficient due to their high values (15 and 16), which could be replaced by a single bit flag, thus conserving bits in the 64-bit ops_mask.
reviews.raw_body:
FUSE_READ==15 and FUSE_WRITE==16; this is a bit wasteful of ops_mask
bits, of which there are only 64.

--D

reviews.author: Joanne Koong (author)
reviews.summary: Author addressed Darrick Wong's concern about the maximum number of passthrough operations, agreeing that 64 is sufficient and thus no further revision is needed.
reviews.raw_body:
That's a good point but I think we're unlikely to need more than 64
passthrough ops, so I think this should be okay.

Thanks,
Joanne

2026-04-20.patch_summary: This patch prepares the fuse filesystem to pass through inode operations by introducing a new field, ops_mask, in the FUSE_DEV_IOC_BACKING_OPEN ioctl input argument. The ops_mask specifies which inode operations will be passed through to the backing file once it is attached to the fuse inode on lookup. Currently, only regular files with FOPEN_PASSTHROUGH are allowed to have passthrough of read/write/splice/mmap operations, but this patch relaxes these restrictions by allowing other operations and non-regular backing files. However, for now, a filesystem must support FUSE_PASSTHROUGH_INO (one-to-one mapping from fuse inode to backing inode) to set up passthrough of any inode operations.
